mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL values directly increase inflation expectations, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and reducing the present value of future corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months with rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack triggers immediate profit margin compression fears and recession probability spikes, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST signals robust consumer spending and housing market strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses explanatory power (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a decline in INDPRO indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, forcing equity valuations down and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in >58% of observations, invalidating the supply-contraction valuation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in PAYEMS reflect growing employment and wage income, which expands aggregate consumption and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < -0.04) over a 5-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in <45% of months with rising employment.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0%, liquidity conditions become excessively accommodative, triggering speculative equity positioning and rapid positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in >60% of periods where FEDFUNDS stays below 1.0%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where GDPC1 growth outpaces CPIAUCSL indicates real economic expansion without inflationary pressure, supporting equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-CPIAUCSL spread shows no predictive power for 20-day returns (t-stat < 1.5) or yields negative returns in >55% of positive-spread quarters.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, rising UNRATE combined with sticky wages compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation periods with rising unemployment, or the margin compression channel fails to materialize in earnings data.
